Center: Center of Nuclear Physics Data, Russian Federal Nuclear Center (VNIIEF), Sarov, Russia
ref: Jour: Nucl. Instrum. Methods in Physics Res., Sect.B, Vol.136-138, p.55 (1998)
title: Parameterization of nuclear reactions cross section using R-matrix theory.

x4freetext: Parameterization of nuclear reactions cross section
using R-matrix theory.

x4codes.shortRef: Jour: Nucl. Instrum. Methods in Physics Res., Sect.B, Vol.136-138, p.55 (1998)

x4freetext: The target consists of a 8 ug/cm2 SiO2 layer
evaporated onto a 98 nm C backing.
Assuming that the alpha scattering by Si is Rutherford
up to 3.8 MeV, the absolute cross section was deduced
from the ratio of the oxygen and the silicon peak
areas. The calibration was made by adjusting current
data to Leavitt et al. (see REL-REF) measurements at
the 3032 keV resonance energy.
The experimental results were corrected on a Si
and O migration into the C backing.
